_meta.title: Phase Capstone — A Raft-Replicated KV Store with Snapshotting

questions.question: Why are snapshots needed in long-running Raft clusters?
options: To compact old log entries and bound replay time
To elect leaders faster than heartbeats
To bypass quorum requirements
To avoid writing state machines
questions.explanation: Snapshots compact committed history so recovery and storage stay bounded.

questions.question: What is the relationship between Raft's log and the state machine?
options: The log is the state machine
The log contains commands; applying them to the state machine produces the current state
The state machine generates the log
They are independent and synchronized periodically
questions.explanation: Raft replicates a log of commands. Each node applies committed log entries to its local state machine deterministically. The state machine is the application's data; the log is the sequence of operations that produced it.

questions.question: When can a follower safely install a snapshot from the leader?
options: When snapshot metadata is newer than follower's compacted index
Only when follower has no log entries
Only after forcing a new election
Only if term numbers are equal to 0
questions.explanation: Install when snapshot carries a sufficiently new lastIncludedIndex/term boundary.

questions.question: Why does the leader send InstallSnapshot instead of just replaying log entries to a lagging follower?
options: InstallSnapshot is faster than AppendEntries
The leader may have already compacted the entries the follower needs, so the log gap cannot be filled from the log alone
InstallSnapshot requires fewer network round trips
AppendEntries doesn't work for lagging followers
questions.explanation: After the leader compacts its log via snapshotting, entries before the snapshot's lastIncludedIndex are discarded. A follower whose log starts before that point cannot receive those entries via AppendEntries — they no longer exist on the leader. InstallSnapshot transfers the state machine snapshot directly, allowing the follower to catch up.

questions.question: What invariant must hold when a leader takes a snapshot?
options: All followers must be online
The snapshot must include all committed entries up to the snapshot index, and the lastIncludedIndex must be committed (replicated to a majority)
The leader must first replicate the snapshot to all followers
The snapshot must be taken at a term boundary
questions.explanation: A snapshot only compacts committed entries — entries that have been replicated to a majority and applied to the state machine. The lastIncludedIndex in the snapshot metadata must correspond to a committed entry. Taking a snapshot of uncommitted entries would risk losing data if the leader fails and a new leader is elected with a different log prefix.
